text: We suffer more often in imagination than in reality.
author: Seneca

text: He who fears death will never do anything worthy of a living man.
author: Seneca

text: Luck is what happens when preparation meets opportunity.
author: Seneca

text: Difficulties strengthen the mind, as labor does the body.
author: Seneca

text: Begin at once to live, and count each separate day as a separate life.
author: Seneca

text: He suffers more than necessary, who suffers before it is necessary.
author: Seneca

text: No man was ever wise by chance.
author: Seneca

text: Associate with people who are likely to improve you.
author: Seneca

text: The best revenge is not to be like your enemy.
author: Marcus Aurelius

text: You have power over your mind — not outside events.
author: Marcus Aurelius

text: The happiness of your life depends upon the quality of your thoughts.
author: Marcus Aurelius

text: Waste no more time arguing what a good man should be. Be one.
author: Marcus Aurelius

text: Everything we hear is an opinion, not a fact.
author: Marcus Aurelius

text: If it is not right, do not do it.
author: Marcus Aurelius

text: Very little is needed to make a happy life.
author: Marcus Aurelius

text: The impediment to action advances action.
author: Marcus Aurelius

text: First say to yourself what you would be; then do what you have to do.
author: Epictetus

text: It’s not what happens to you, but how you react to it that matters.
author: Epictetus

text: Wealth consists not in having great possessions, but in having few wants.
author: Epictetus

text: Make the best use of what is in your power.
author: Epictetus

text: No great thing is created suddenly.
author: Epictetus

text: Freedom is the only worthy goal in life.
author: Epictetus

text: The greater the difficulty, the more glory in surmounting it.
author: Epictetus

text: Circumstances don’t make the man, they only reveal him.
author: Epictetus

text: Man conquers the world by conquering himself.
author: Zeno of Citium

text: If a man knows not to which port he sails, no wind is favorable.
author: Seneca

text: True happiness is to enjoy the present.
author: Seneca

text: He is most powerful who has power over himself.
author: Seneca

text: The whole future lies in uncertainty: live immediately.
author: Seneca

text: To bear trials with a calm mind robs misfortune of its strength.
author: Seneca

text: No person has the power to have everything they want.
author: Seneca

text: We become wiser by adversity.
author: Seneca

text: It is not that we have a short time to live, but that we waste much of it.
author: Seneca

text: Life is long if you know how to use it.
author: Seneca

text: The mind that is anxious about future events is miserable.
author: Seneca

text: Look well into yourself; there is a source of strength.
author: Marcus Aurelius

text: The soul becomes dyed with the color of its thoughts.
author: Marcus Aurelius

text: Confine yourself to the present.
author: Marcus Aurelius

text: Accept the things to which fate binds you.
author: Marcus Aurelius

text: What stands in the way becomes the way.
author: Marcus Aurelius

text: Do every act of your life as if it were your last.
author: Marcus Aurelius

text: Be tolerant with others and strict with yourself.
author: Marcus Aurelius

text: External things are not the problem.
author: Marcus Aurelius

text: The universe is change; our life is what our thoughts make it.
author: Marcus Aurelius

text: Loss is nothing else but change.
author: Marcus Aurelius

text: Don't demand that things happen as you wish.
author: Epictetus

text: No man is free who is not master of himself.
author: Epictetus

text: We are disturbed not by things, but by the views we take of them.
author: Epictetus

text: Attach yourself to what is spiritually superior.
author: Epictetus

text: Difficulties show what men are.
author: Epictetus

text: If you want to improve, be content to be thought foolish.
author: Epictetus

text: Practice yourself in little things.
author: Epictetus

text: Freedom is secured not by the fulfilling of desires.
author: Epictetus

text: Only the educated are free.
author: Epictetus

text: It is impossible for a man to learn what he thinks he already knows.
author: Epictetus

text: Fate leads the willing and drags the unwilling.
author: Cleanthes

text: Reason is the commanding faculty of the soul.
author: Chrysippus

text: Virtue is sufficient for happiness.
author: Zeno of Citium

text: Follow nature.
author: Zeno of Citium

text: The wise man is self-sufficient.
author: Zeno of Citium

text: He who is brave is free.
author: Seneca

text: Anger, if not restrained, is frequently more hurtful than the injury that provokes it.
author: Seneca

text: Time heals what reason cannot.
author: Seneca

text: It is the power of the mind to be unconquerable.
author: Seneca

text: A gem cannot be polished without friction.
author: Seneca

text: The bravest sight in the world is to see a great man struggling against adversity.
author: Seneca

text: Fire tests gold; adversity tests brave men.
author: Seneca

text: Nothing is more honorable than a grateful heart.
author: Seneca

text: True happiness is... to enjoy the present.
author: Seneca

text: Wherever there is a human being, there is an opportunity for kindness.
author: Seneca

text: The best answer to anger is silence.
author: Marcus Aurelius

text: Today I escaped anxiety.
author: Marcus Aurelius

text: You could leave life right now.
author: Marcus Aurelius

text: Do not act as if you were going to live ten thousand years.
author: Marcus Aurelius

text: Be like the cliff against which the waves break.
author: Marcus Aurelius

text: Nothing happens to anyone that he is not fitted by nature to bear.
author: Marcus Aurelius

text: When you arise in the morning, think of what a privilege it is to be alive.
author: Marcus Aurelius

text: The art of living is more like wrestling than dancing.
author: Marcus Aurelius

text: How much more grievous are the consequences of anger than the causes of it.
author: Marcus Aurelius

text: Everything that happens is either endurable or not.
author: Marcus Aurelius

text: Seek not the good in external things.
author: Epictetus

text: Know first who you are, and then adorn yourself accordingly.
author: Epictetus

text: If you wish to be a writer, write.
author: Epictetus

text: Caretake this moment.
author: Epictetus

text: Nature hath given men one tongue but two ears.
author: Epictetus

text: Be not diverted from your duty.
author: Epictetus

text: The key is to keep company only with people who uplift you.
author: Epictetus

text: When something happens, the only thing in your power is your attitude.
author: Epictetus

text: Small-minded people blame others.
author: Epictetus

text: Control thy passions lest they take vengeance on thee.
author: Epictetus

text: Contentment is natural wealth.
author: Zeno of Citium

text: Better to trip with the feet than with the tongue.
author: Zeno of Citium

text: Happiness is a good flow of life.
author: Zeno of Citium

text: Man is disturbed not by events but by his opinion about them.
author: Epictetus

text: To live happily is an inward power of the soul.
author: Marcus Aurelius

text: He who is contented is rich.
author: Seneca

text: We learn not in the school, but in life.
author: Seneca

text: The first step towards amendment is the recognition of error.
author: Seneca

text: While we wait for life, life passes.
author: Seneca

text: Nothing endures but change.
author: Heraclitus

text: If someone despises me, that is their problem.
author: Marcus Aurelius

text: Do not explain your philosophy. Embody it.
author: Epictetus

text: Be silent or let thy words be worth more than silence.
author: Zeno of Citium

text: He who is everywhere is nowhere.
author: Seneca

text: It is quality rather than quantity that matters.
author: Seneca

text: Live according to nature.
author: Zeno of Citium

text: What need is there to weep over parts of life?
author: Seneca

text: Don’t seek for everything to happen as you wish.
author: Epictetus

text: The more we value things outside our control, the less control we have.
author: Epictetus

text: Throw me to the wolves and I will return leading the pack.
author: Seneca

text: Receive without pride, let go without attachment.
author: Marcus Aurelius

text: If it is endurable, then endure it.
author: Marcus Aurelius

text: He who lives in harmony with himself lives in harmony with the universe.
author: Marcus Aurelius

text: Settle on the type of person you want to be.
author: Epictetus

text: The willing are led by fate.
author: Cleanthes
